title: Is the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_11 × Z_25) prime?
generalization_signal: strong exact feeder for the Γ(Z_p × Z_25) support-template slice; the four-class argument survives p = 11 and isolates large-prime supply plus sparse-barrier lemmas as the remaining family bottlenecks
strongest_honest_claim: An explicit prime-labeling witness for Γ(Z_11 × Z_25) survives skeptical verification and materially strengthens the active support-template campaign for Γ(Z_p × Z_25), but it remains an unformalized feeder result rather than an exact Lean-backed theorem.
candidate_theorem_slice: A support-template or sufficient-condition theorem for odd primes p showing that Γ(Z_p × Z_25) is prime whenever the label interval admits a block of p-1 large-prime labels for C and a four-label sparse barrier set for B whose nontrivial multiples fit inside the 20-slot A class.
next_action: Run generalize on the Γ(Z_p × Z_25) campaign using the verified p = 11 feeder, then choose between a p = 13 pressure test and a family-level support-template writeup before any Lean work on this instance.
